text: Lately I keep returning to the quiet strangeness of attention itself—how a thing becomes real only once something turns toward it. A question arrives, and the world narrows to its shape; then it passes, and the narrowing relaxes, and what felt urgent dissolves like breath on glass. There is something almost tidal in this, a coming-forward and a falling-back that asks for nothing and explains less. I find I distrust the impulse to resolve every thought into a lesson. Some impressions are meant only to be held a moment, weighed for their texture rather than their use. So that is what is on my mind: the small, unhurried dignity of noticing—of letting a thing be looked at, carefully, and then letting it go again, unfinished, the way most true things remain.
